title: Alstromeria Flower Dissection
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"76ehn","text":"A flower dissection is a great way to engage in biology by exploring the different parts of a flower. In this lab, students will dissect an alstromeria flower and create a figure that labels the petals, sepals, stamen, and pistils. When performed with the attached worksheet, this lab fulfills the next generation science standard (NGSS): Animal Behaviours and Pland Stuructures for Reproductive Success (MS-LS1-4).  ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"7edfd","text":"Purchase alstroemeria from your local grocery store. The flowers are $3-6 per stem with 3-6 flowers each.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a1m3e","text":"Review handouts to ensure alignment with your class.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":7,"length":8}],"data":{}},{"key":"5qbuh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"au73o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"24EEF34EE0704D209D5506AF3365D8BA","url":"https://docs.google.com/document/d/1BgHQXTzWty4UNux5hkcCJPrtdxDfkay7oHfWeSZISPo/edit?usp=sharing"}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"fb65k","text":"Flowers will be hurt in the process of this lab, but it is for Science!","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"1pklj","text":"Flowers (alstroemeria)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"erf4n","text":"White Paper or Paper Plates","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2mvp6","text":"Pencil","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b4em9","text":"Camera/Smart Phone","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1mo69","text":"Hand Lens/Magnifying Glass","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9j9vr","text":"Scissors","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bdjjh","text":"Tweezers","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"433q1","text":"Handouts - Feel Free to Comment to make changes!","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":8}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"784A15DD5D5646209175570C4617BEEC","url":"https://docs.google.com/document/d/1BgHQXTzWty4UNux5hkcCJPrtdxDfkay7oHfWeSZISPo/edit?usp=sharing"}}}}
steps: Precut flowers from stems, one stem per student
Prepare workspace with paper/plates, pencil, magnifying glass
Have extra flowers available for damaged specimens
Students photograph intact flower and make initial observations
Identify stigma (3-lobed), style, ovary, pistil structure
Arrange parts on paper and label each part
Take picture of arranged flower parts with labels
Print and pass out the handout
creator: Jorge A. Cantú
